h2h_summary: Ivory Coast and Ecuador have never previously met in international football — their Group E clash on June 14, 2026 will be the first-ever encounter between the two nations.
wc_history_home: Ivory Coast appeared at the World Cup in 2006, 2010, and 2014 but were eliminated in the group stage each time, and the 2026 tournament marks their return after a 12-year absence.
wc_history_away: Ecuador have appeared at the World Cup in 2002, 2006, and 2014, with their best run coming in 2006 when they reached the Round of 16 before losing 1-0 to England.
group_context: Group E comprises Germany, Ecuador, Ivory Coast, and Curaçao; Germany are heavy favourites to top the group, making the Ivory Coast vs Ecuador opener a near-decisive contest for second place, with Ecuador given a roughly 87% chance and Ivory Coast around 64% of advancing to the knockouts.
strengths_home: Explosive, pacey wide attackers led by Amad Diallo and Yan Diomande
Defensive solidity — conceded zero goals across 10 World Cup qualifying matches
Youngest squad at the tournament with momentum from recent wins over France and South Korea
strengths_away: Best defence in CONMEBOL qualifying — only five goals conceded in 18 matches
Moises Caicedo as one of the world's elite defensive midfielders anchoring a disciplined shape
Dangerous on the counter-attack with Valencia, Paez, and Gonzalo Plata providing the threat in transition
team_style_home: Ivory Coast deploy a 4-3-3 under Emerse Fae, pressing high and transitioning quickly through pace-filled wide attackers, underpinned by a physically dominant midfield and a defensive unit that conceded zero goals in World Cup qualifying.
team_style_away: Ecuador under Sebastian Beccacece are built on elite defensive organisation in a compact 4-4-2 / 4-2-3-1 shape that conceded just five goals in 18 CONMEBOL qualifiers, relying on Caicedo's engine in midfield and devastating counter-attacks to punish opponents.
recent_news: Ivory Coast and Ecuador face off in a pivotal Group E fixture at the 2026 World Cup, with both teams vying for second place behind Germany. The match is predicted to end in a 1-1 draw, with both teams having scored in three of Ecuador's last four outings.
context: Group E comprises Germany, Ecuador, Ivory Coast, and Curaçao; Germany are heavy favourites to top the group, making the Ivory Coast vs Ecuador opener a near-decisive contest for second place, with Ecuador given a roughly 87% chance and Ivory Coast around 64% of advancing to the knockouts.
